type_reps.read_via: resolve(); returned as raw str, no coercion
type_reps.notes: Pure str pass-through of os.getenv(key, default). Byte-identical characterization: unset->default, set->override.
Registry stores str; call site coerces with int(). Malformed value -> ValueError raised at call site (module import time). Empty-string value would also raise.
Threshold keys. float() coercion at call site; malformed -> ValueError.
Boolean via =="1" comparison at call site. NEVER raises: any non-'1' value (malformed, empty, unset->'0') yields False. Only literal '1' is True.

type_reps.read_via: ModelSettings field via get_model_settings() (pydantic, validation_alias, case_sensitive=True); PG_JUDGE_MODEL is str non-empty default, PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL is 'str | None' with None default
type_reps.notes: Distinct from resolve() layer. 12 model keys total. Case-sensitive: lowercase alias (pg_judge_model) is IGNORED. Fresh instance each call = live read. PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL also present in CONFIG_DEFAULTS with None default (dual-registered).

type_reps.read_via: resolve() as plain str/None -- NOT SecretStr
type_reps.notes: KEY/TOKEN/SECRET-shaped keys are plain strings, no masking. OPENROUTER_API_KEY & EXA_API_KEY have '' default; ZYTE_API_KEY has None default (returns None when unset).

Empty-string default: resolve() returns '' when unset (falsy sentinel meaning 'auto/disabled'). PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL & PG_POLICY_MODEL ALSO exist as ModelSettings fields with '' default (dual-registered str).

keys: <any key not in CONFIG_DEFAULTS, e.g. PG_TOTALLY_UNREGISTERED_KEY_XYZ>
type_reps.notes: resolve() raises KeyError for a key not present in CONFIG_DEFAULTS (intentional: multi-line/computed/conflicting defaults excluded, migrated manually). Verified empirically.

axes_applicable: value-state: empty-string (returns '' for str keys; ValueError for int/float-coerced call sites; False for bool =="1")
value-state: valid (override read live from env)
value-state: malformed (str: passes through raw; int/float: ValueError at call site; bool: silently False)
precedence: registry-default vs process-env override (os.getenv live read; process env wins)
precedence: .env layer EXISTS via load_dotenv() default override=False -- process env takes precedence over .env, .env over registry default
precedence: NO dedicated CLI layer in config module (a few scripts set os.environ at startup = plain process-env, not a resolve()/ModelSettings precedence tier)
key-case-sensitivity: ModelSettings uses case_sensitive=True (lowercase alias ignored); resolve()/os.getenv is inherently case-sensitive
read-timing: resolve() reads live env EVERY call; ModelSettings SNAPSHOTS env at construction, but get_model_settings() returns a FRESH instance per call => effectively live
runtime-type: str (resolve plain) vs coerced-at-callsite (int/float/bool) vs pydantic-typed (ModelSettings str / str|None). NO SecretStr type in this codebase
result.baseline: 16738 tests collected, 11 collection errors (via /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest --collect-only at /home/polaris/wt/phase-1b on branch chore/review-readiness-1b). Errors are pre-existing collection failures (playwright TargetClosedError, mesh/benchmark import errors) unrelated to config. Worktree created, oracle overlaid by copy (tests/oracle/*.py + cassettes/*). 871 keys in CONFIG_DEFAULTS, 12 model keys in MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS. No SecretStr anywhere in src/. Coercion (int/float/==\"1\") happens at call sites, not in the config layer."

result.notes:
Wrote /home/polaris/wt/phase-1b/tests/test_config_characterization_matrix.py. 118 tests, all PASS (0.15s), hermetic (monkeypatch env only, no network/fs). Command: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_config_characterization_matrix.py -q | tail -3 -> "118 passed".

Coverage across all 8 representative axes x value-state x precedence:
- Core identity: every representative key asserts resolve(key) == os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]) byte-for-byte across {unset->registry default, valid override via monkeypatch.setenv, empty '', malformed}. Malformed/empty pass through raw at the resolve() layer (resolve NEVER raises on a value).
- Axis A (plain str): no coercion, no stripping; type is exactly str.
- Axis B (int, state.py:23/:44): int(resolve(k)) parses unset default + valid; malformed AND empty '' raise ValueError at the CALL SITE (documented resolve() itself does not raise).
- Axis C (float, state.py:33 / cross_reference.py:47): same pattern, float() raises on malformed + '' at call site.
- Axis D (bool ==\"1\", checkpoint_manager.py:26 / state.py:80): NEVER raises; only literal '1' is True; parametrized 10 non-'1' values (incl. '', 'true', ' 1', '01', malformed) all False; verified opposite defaults (CHECKPOINT '0'->False, AMPLIFICATION '1'->True).
- Axis E (model keys, ModelSettings pydantic): get_model_settings().field for unset default (judge='qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b', evaluator=None) + valid override; case_sensitive=True asserted (lowercase pg_judge_model IGNORED, correct-case wins); PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL dual-registration (CONFIG_DEFAULTS None + ModelSettings None) agrees across both layers.
- Axis F (secret-shaped): asserts type(val) is str (NOT SecretStr) and value exposed verbatim, no masking -- documents the pre-SecretStr baseline so a future SecretStr pass must consciously update it. Defaults: OPENROUTER/EXA '' , ZYTE None.
- Axis G (empty-string default): '' sentinel when unset.
- Axis H (unregistered key): resolve() raises KeyError even when the env var IS set (registry is the gate, not env).
- Read-timing/freshness: resolve() reads live env on every call (set-after-import seen); get_model_settings() returns a fresh non-cached instance per call, old snapshot not mutated.
- Precedence: process-env strictly beats registry default for every representative; documented there is NO dedicated CLI tier inside resolve()/ModelSettings.

All assertions LOCK current behaviour (characterization, not specification).

codex_points: .env precedence layer is UNTESTED: asserting process-env > registry-default does NOT prove .env > registry-default or process-env > .env. A regression in dotenv loading or the override=False setting could go undetected. Must independently lock both .env-beats-registry and process-env-beats-.env.

ModelSettings needs explicit empty-value coverage: str|None typing does not establish whether an empty '' env value stays empty, becomes None, or is ignored. 'Malformed' is not formally defined for unconstrained strings — either mark it N/A or add a test asserting a malformed-looking value is preserved verbatim.

resolve()-layer key-case is NOT locked: case_sensitivity is proven for ModelSettings (lowercase alias ignored) but the evidence does not lock resolve()/registry key casing. Add a test using a lowercase form of a registered key to prove the registry remains case-sensitive.

The existing assertions ARE genuine characterization (not aspirational): they preserve current exceptions (int/float ValueError, KeyError), coercion, literal bool ==\"1\" semantics, live-read freshness, and the absence of SecretStr.

oracle_matches=false is NOT intrinsically a behavioral blocker — it is only a procedural blocker IF exact oracle matching is an explicit gate requirement for this initiative. Confirm whether the deterministic-oracle match is a hard gate; if so, resolve oracle_sha before merge.
Gate outcome: 1B-REVISE. Add three axes before this can be 1B-OK: (1) .env precedence tier, (2) ModelSettings empty/pass-through value-state, (3) resolve-layer key-case sensitivity.

Operational note: the first codex run timed out at 200s and was terminated (exit 143, output 'Terminated'); the verdict above came from a retry at 280s. The medium-effort run completed in ~8.5K tokens.
